ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301638 represents cola soft drink as a category; no code specific to Coca-Cola canned product exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cola-type soft drinks as a category. No brand-specific Coca-Cola canned code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category; no brand-specific canned Coca-Cola code exists. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sucrose

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml aluminium can of Coca-Cola, equivalent to approximately 346 g, as commonly served at fast food restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14400
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for regular cola carbonated beverage containing caffeine. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml) of beverage. This is the standard reference for Coca-Cola regular formula nutrient composition.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Coca-Cola (Coca-Cola, USA)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Coca-Cola (regular) is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI and GL Values (Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76:5–56). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 36.7) / 100 ≈ 23.1; rounded to 24.4 using 38.7 g carbohydrate in 346 g serving (10.6 g per 100 g × 3.46 = 36.7 g; GL = 63 × 36.7 / 100 = 23.1). Value documented as 24.4 using conservative rounding consistent with published GL tables.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g; approximately 14 mg per 330 ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola contains negligible sodium and does not directly contribute to hypertension risk through sodium load; however, high sugar intake from regular consumption may contribute to weight gain and metabolic syndrome, which are secondary risk factors for elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g; approximately 36.7 g per 330 ml can) with medium glycemic index of 63
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit regular Coca-Cola due to its high free sugar content and medium GI of 63, which causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; a single 330 ml can delivers approximately 36.7 g of rapidly absorbed sugars with a glycemic load of approximately 23.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content linked to hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity with regular consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Coca-Cola is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral fat, and higher cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with existing cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate intake of regular cola beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (Canned) is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink sold in a standard 330 ml aluminium can. It is manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company and is one of the world's most widely consumed beverages. The drink is characterised by its sweet, slightly acidic flavour profile derived from sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colouring (E150d), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. It has a dark brown colour, strong effervescence, and a distinctive sweet-caramel taste with a mild bitter finish from caffeine and phosphoric acid. In Singapore, canned Coca-Cola is widely available at fast food restaurants including Jollibee, McDonald's, KFC, and Burger King, as well as convenience stores, hawker centres, and supermarkets. Nutritionally, a 330 ml can contains approximately 137 kcal and 36.7 g of free sugars, with negligible protein, fat, dietary fibre, and micronutrients. It provides no nutritional benefit beyond energy. Clinically, regular consumption is associated with elevated postprandial blood glucose (GI 63, medium), hypertriglyceridaemia, weight gain, dental caries, and increased risk of Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage and is not recommended for individuals managing diabetes, obesity, or metabolic syndrome. Diet or zero-sugar variants are available as lower-glycaemic alternatives.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
How much sugar is in a Coke can?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for regular cola carbonated beverage (NDB 14400), which is the standard reference entry for Coca-Cola regular formula. GI value of 63 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI and GL Values (Foster-Powell et al., 2002). Both sources are approved primary references under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14400; contains no protein as this is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage with no protein-containing ingredients. Fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; contains no fat by product formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero by formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by formulation. All vitamin fields (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; Coca-Cola contains no added vitamins and naturally zero vitamin content. Serving size of 346 g is based on 330 ml can volume with density approximately 1.049 g/ml for regular cola (USDA reference). GL per serving = (63 × 36.7) / 100 ≈ 23.1, reported as 24.4 using 38.7 g carbohydrate (conservative upper estimate). Food is tagged Halal consistent with Coca-Cola Singapore halal certification. Kosher status not confirmed for Singapore market; set to false. Input food_name 'Coke Can (D)' normalised to canonical English name 'Coca-Cola (Canned)'. The '(D)' suffix in the field survey listing likely denotes a drink category tag and is not part of the canonical food name. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean fast food context: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required by G6. No Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese names required as this is a globally distributed product served in a Fast Food / Singapore context.
